title: tanacetum capitatum
intro: Tanacetum capitatum, also known as Tansy, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to treat a range of health issues. The medicinal properties of Tansy are used to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as gas, bloating, and indigestion, as well as to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Tansy can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and reduce stress.
